NACE sector: Transmission of electricity
TAC rationale: The interconnected European System of transmission and distribution of electricity meets the criteria (source: JRC study). The only portion of the activity that is not aligned are those related to the direct connection of carbon intensive power plants to the grid. At this stage, we are not able to provide an estimate for this portion, which is expected to be small compared to the whole activity (also in terms of Km of transmission lines). Therefore, we set the TAC to 100%.
TEC rationale: Complement to 1 of TAC.

NACE sector: Distribution of electricity
TAC rationale: The interconnected European System of transmission and distribution of electricity meets the criteria defined in the column 'Metrics and Threshold" (source: JRC study). The only portion of the activity that is not aligned are those related to the direct connection from e.g. carbon intensive power plants to the grid. At this stage, we are not able to provide an estimate for this portion, which is expected to be small compared to the whole activity (also in terms of Km of transmission lines). Therefore, in 2020 we set the TAC to 100%.
TEC rationale: Complement to 1 of TAC.

NACE sector: Manufacture of gas
TAC rationale: "Currently, biogas and biomethane projects represent only a small fraction of total global spending on gas. Investments have averaged less than USD 4 billion per year over the last decade, the same amount the natural gas industry typically spends every week. In the STEPS, annual spending on biogas and biomethane rises more than threefold to reach around USD 14 billion by 2040. [...] The share of biomethane and biogas in total investment spending on gas remains well below 5%" (2020 data, source IEA:https://iea.blob.core.windows.net/assets/03aeb10c-c38c-4d10-bcec-de92e9ab815f/Outlook_for_biogas_and_biomethane.pdf)
TEC rationale: Complement to 1 of TAC. The revenues of this NACE sector currently depend almost entirely from extraction, manufacturing or sale of fossil fuel.

NACE sector: Steam and air conditioning supply
TAC rationale:
Renewable energy (incl. derived heat) accounts for 21% of the total energy used for heating and cooling in the European Union (EU) (2018 data, source Eurostat: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/products-eurostat-news/-/ddn-20200211-1).
For the TAC activity, we make following considerations. ‘Efficient district heating and cooling’ means a district heating or cooling system using at least 50 % renewable energy, 50 % waste heat, 75 % cogenerated heat or 50 % of a combination of such energy and heat (Directive 2012/27/EU). In 2018, renewable energy (incl. derived heat) accounted for 21% of the total energy used for heating and cooling in the European Union (EU) (souce Eurostat: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/products-eurostat-news/-/ddn-20200211-1). The TAC of the activity corresponds to the mean between the following two scenarios: 1) aligned installations are all using a share of 100% renewables, in this case the TAC would be 21%, and 2) aligned installations are all using a share of 50% renewables, in this case the TAC would be 42%. The resulting TAC activity is (21%+42%)/2 = 31.5%.
TEC rationale: Share of the production of electricity and derived heat from fossil fuels in the EU (2019 data, source Eurostat: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/databrowser/view/NRG_BAL_PEH__custom_1160933/default/table?lang=en)

NACE sector: Treatment and disposal of non-hazardous waste

Across the EU, between 118 and 138 million tons of bio-waste arise annually, of which currently only about 40% is effectively recycled into high-quality compost and digestate (source: https://www.compostnetwork.info/policy/biowaste-in-europe). Hence, a TAC of 0.022.

NACE sector: Construction of buildings
TAC rationale:
The TAC for NACE F41  is conservatively set equal to the lower between the TAC Activity for construction of new buildings and the TAC Activity for renovation of existing buildings. 
To estimate the TAC Activity for renovation, we define the following two measures: Old Net Primary Energy Demand (oldNPED) and New Net Primary Energy Demand (newNPED), which refer to the NPED of the building abefore and after renovation, respectively.  It follows that the improvement of NPED called imprNPED = - (newNPED - oldNPED)/oldNPED. We assume that imprNPED is uniformly distributed between 0% and 50%. An activity is Taxonomy aligned if the imprNPED of the renovated building is at least 30%. It follows that TAC Activity = (0.5 - 0.3)/0.5 =  40%.
TEC rationale: The SH threshold in the Climate DA Annex 2 is the NZEB requirement, which is mandatory for new buildings.

NACE sector: Development of building projects
TAC rationale:
The TAC for NACE F41 is conservatively set equal to the lower between the TAC Activity for construction of new buildings and the TAC Activity for renovation of existing buildings. 
The TAC Activity for new buildings is estimated as follows. An activity is Taxonomy aligned if the NPED of the new construction is at least 10% lower than the NZEB. We assume that the metric used, i.e. net Primary Energy Demand (NPED), which measures energy efficiency of new buildings,  is uniformly distributed between 50% and 100% of the Near Zero-Energy Buildings (NZEB) requirements (expressed in kWh/m2). Under this assumption it follows that TAC = (0.9 - 0.5)/0.5 =  80%.
TEC rationale: This NACE sector does not belong to the list of sectors defined as highly exposed to transition risk.

NACE sector: Construction of residential and non-residential buildings
TAC rationale:
The TAC for NACE F41 is conservatively set equal to the lower between the TAC Activity for construction of new buildings and the TAC Activity for renovation of existing buildings. 
The TAC Activity for new buildings is estimated as follows. An activity is Taxonomy aligned if the NPED of the new construction is at least 10% lower than the NZEB. We assume that the metric used, i.e. net Primary Energy Demand (NPED), which measures energy efficiency of new buildings,  is uniformly distributed between 50% and 100% of the Near Zero-Energy Buildings (NZEB) requirements (expressed in kWh/m2). Under this assumption it follows that TAC = (0.9 - 0.5)/0.5 =  80%.
TEC rationale: The SH threshold in the Climate DA Annex 2 is the NZEB requirement, which is mandatory for new buildings.

The TEC for NACE F42.22 is obtained by multiplying this share by the share of electricity production based on fossil fuels (TEC for NACE D35.11)

NACE sector: Specialised construction activities
TAC rationale:
The TAC for NACE F43 is conservatively set equal to the lower between the TAC Activity for construction of new buildings and the TAC Activity for renovation of existing buildings. 
The TAC Activity for new buildings is estimated as follows. An activity is Taxonomy aligned if the NPED of the new construction is at least 10% lower than the NZEB. We assume that the metric used, i.e. net Primary Energy Demand (NPED), which measures energy efficiency of new buildings,  is uniformly distributed between 50% and 100% of the Near Zero-Energy Buildings (NZEB) requirements (expressed in kWh/m2). Under this assumption it follows that TAC = (0.9 - 0.5)/0.5 =  80%.
TEC rationale: This NACE sector does not belong to the list of sectors defined as highly exposed to transition risk.
